Name: Chum
Description_EN: Offered up by Chums when planting themselves into the ground. Perfectly smooth and hard as a rock, these eggs seem to float with how light they are. There must be a good place to deliver these.

Name: YellowElephantBeetle
Description_EN: Excellent at escaping would be predators, Yellow Elephant Beetles distinct fondness for Tassel Seeds is surprisingly effective at distracting them enough for capture.

Name: Calibrator
Description_EN: Some sort of ancient calibration device Sizo asked me to retrieve from a ruined Atomic ship.

Name: PowerSupply
Description_EN: This device supplies power to whatever it's plugged into. Sizo will have to show me how it works.

Name: ControlPanel
Description_EN: An internal control device, used for allowing a machine to monitor its own mechanisms and make adjustments where needed. Without one of these in a bike, you’re better off riding a goat.

Name: Navigator
Description_EN: An ancient measuring device, used to mark positions on maps over long distances, a useful tool when mapping one's own path across unfamiliar terrain.

Name: GlidingStone01
Description_EN: I can feel a deep vibration at its core as I hold it in my hand. It is an impenetrable darkness but its surface has barely perceptible markings etched into it.

Name: GlidingStone02
Description_EN: Light pulses through the dark rock, somehow it acts as a lens to focus the Perpetual energy around us. It gives off a warm energy when used.

Name: AtomicHeartKeycard
Description_EN: A keycard given to me by the Eccrine Guard Sandip. It allows me access to the Atomic Heart, the structure that provides power to Eccria.

Name: OasisFlower
Description_EN: A beautiful flower that grows atop a rock in the Eccrine Oasis. A gift to give someone you care about.

Name: LarvalHusk
Description_EN: The remaining husks of metamorphosed Hercules Beetles, often found in the center of their nests. Extremely tough, useful material for creating tough rigid clothing items or pieces of architecture.

Name: SlicerBeetlePoo
Description_EN: Apparently valuable as a fuel and fertiliser, slicer beetles need some encouragement to let these go sometimes.

Name: OrangeWingBeetle
Description_EN: A skittish, wary creature that survives cautiously in the shaded geodesic rocks of the Wash. They are quick to escape in soft sands but their short mandibles are not suited to cutting harder materials.

Name: BasicFishingRod
Description_EN: An old wooden rod, it's just about serviceable. I won't be catching anything big with this thing.

Name: Glowworm
Description_EN: Fluorescent worms found on the ceiling of a cave behind a waterfall of sulphur in Hakoa. They drop from their stalactites if you smoke them out using dissolving rocks found in the caves.Small sandwyrms love to eat them but they aren’t known for being good for humans.

Name: NimoorButterflies
Description_EN: They hunt around for Nimoor plants at night and when they find one, crawl inside the core to give it a glowing radiance as they consume nutrients from the plant.

Name: GlowingMushrooms
Description_EN: Glowing mushrooms found in the glow worm caves, said to give off a certain buzz when consumed.

Name: StyllousNote
Description_EN: A note taken from the Petrified Wyrm.

Name: MachinistBadge
Description_EN: Shaped like some sort of mechanical part, the badge feels solid and well engineered in my palm.

Name: LightningCrystal
Description_EN: Lightning infused crystals that grow out of the sides of floating rocks in the Crystal Plateau. Extremely risky to harvest, the power inside each crystal is extremely powerful and volatile. Cracking one can be dangerous.

Name: GuardsBadge
Description_EN: A feather from a bird of prey, given as a badge by the guard Eliisabet.

Name: AnAncientRaceKeyItem
Description_EN: A strange, circular ring that I retrieved from a shrine buried under the sand. I can feel a light tug towards an unknown place when I hold it lightly in my hands. Like a key that belongs somewhere.

Name: BeetleBadge
Description_EN: Shaped like a Beetle's mandibles this badge given by beetle camp owners.

Name: StormFish
Description_EN: A unique fish that rides the aggressive movement of sand that occurs during a sandstorm by expanding it's fins to an enormous span, as if it were a bird about to take flight.

Name: BellowGuppy
Description_EN: It pulses its way through the sands using an air propulsion system that grows at the end of its tail. When frightened, the force of the propulsion can cause the 'bellow' to drop off leaving the fish stranded.

Name: PillFish
Description_EN: A small frilly fish that collect in swarms near cactus roots. They are poisonous to humans but secrete a fluid sometimes used for painkilling purposes.

Name: LittleKicker
Description_EN: This small fish has a split fin that it uses to propel its way through the dunes as if it had two legs kicking back and forth.

Name: SandRay
Description_EN: This rare fish is held up as a sign of good luck in cultures across the dunes. They typically travel alone. A delicacy in Redsee but eating sand rays is frowned upon elsewhere.

Name: TeacupAngler
Description_EN: Somehow lives in the pools of sulphur in Hakoa. They seem to be the only living creature that can survive in such an environment.

Name: ScabbyFish
Description_EN: Enjoys the cooler climes of the Badlands floor, this fish is only remarkable in how unremarkable it is.

Name: TrifleJelly
Description_EN: Unfortunately inedible despite its name, the layers of thin membrane surround a rock-like core which shrinks over the course of its lifetime until it dies.

Name: LightKoi
Description_EN: Native to the Badlands, these beautiful creatures are often kept and sold as pets but their glistening scales are also used in jewelry sold in Eccria.

Name: BloatedEyeClam
Description_EN: Living on the roots of dead trees, their hardened purple exterior is filled with an extremely delicious, soft flesh desired all across Midden.

Name: PebbleEye
Description_EN: It’s thin pencil-like body means these fish can burrow into small crevices and holes in rocks, protecting themselves from the sun and predators.

Name: MonumentalBikeWingsSchematic
Description_EN: A rubbing of a schematic I took from the old Hicaric shrine. If I take this to the engineer outside the shrine they might be able to help me.

Name: MonumentalBikeBoosterSchematic
Description_EN: A rubbing of a schematic I took from the old Hicaric shrine. If I take this to the engineer outside the shrine they might be able to help me.

Name: MonumentalBikeFrontSchematic
Description_EN: A rubbing of a schematic I took from the old Hicaric shrine. If I take this to the engineer outside the shrine they might be able to help me.

Name: AtomicWhaleshipBikeFront
Description_EN: Perfectly preserved in the Whale, the front of this bike is made from familiar looking metals moulded with precision impossible for humans to replicate.

Name: AtomicWhaleshipBikeWings
Description_EN: Kept in the Whale for an incomprehensibly long time. As it switches states the mechanisms glide fluidly in a satisfying, fluid movement.

Name: AtomicWhaleshipBikeBooster
Description_EN: An inaudible vibrating hum emanates from the sleek surface of this part when you put your hands on it. It feels alive to every subtle movement made by the driver.

Name: BeetleFront
Description_EN: Frantic energy runs through the mandible-like features of this bike, never stopping and constantly in motion as if it is looking for something.

Name: BeetleWings
Description_EN: A part often utilised by Beetle enthusiasts, the mandible-like wings flare out with intense tension when riders strafe and curl up tight for tight aerodynamic gliding when driving at high speeds.

Name: BeetleBooster
Description_EN: Smoke pours out of the many exhausts at the back of this booster, best to avoid use indoors or near other people.

Name: DeliveryBikeFront
Description_EN: A part used by delivery drivers across Midden.There are hidden storage slots built underneath metal panels at the front for sensitive cargo.

Name: DeliveryBikeWings
Description_EN: Metal frames on the side of the bike are used for hauling gear by Couriers. Getting the balance of the bike correct before setting off is very important, riding an unsettled bike over a big dune can result in a messy landing.

Name: DeliveryBikeBooster
Description_EN: Carefully sprung and balanced for delivering delicate gear across all types of terrain. It would take a big hit for a package to drop off the back of the carrier.

Name: EyriesBikeFront
Description_EN: The engine is surrounded by lightweight wood and hollow bone, it needs a lot of maintenance to keep running cleanly, but when it does, it's a great ride.

Name: EyriesBikeWings
Description_EN: An exo-skeleton of bone and lightweight wood construction give this a boat-like feeling, carved by an expert craftsman in the Badland Eyries.

Name: EyriesBikeBooster
Description_EN: Red wood from the Uplands of the Badlands combined with bones from the Chales make this a great, if rustic, material to ride with.

Name: GiraffeFront
Description_EN: An engine designed for pulling things across long distances. Commonly used for transporting large ship parts, Scrappers are often seen with these.

Name: GiraffeWings
Description_EN: Stores large amounts of energy for pulling objects across dunes, useful for pulling something covered by heavy sands to the surface.

Name: GiraffeBooster
Description_EN: The tall neck adjusts to counterweight large objects that this bike is used to pull across the dunes. It can feel a little unwieldy if you aren’t towing anything.

Name: GlidingBikeFront
Description_EN: The Eccrine made glass at the core of this part is unmatched in durability. The stability and chemical purity of the glass makes the energy source inside it extremely stable.

Name: GlidingBikeWings
Description_EN: Wings for the bike I helped Sizo to build. They provide a smooth balance of movement, perfect for long journeys.

Name: GlidingBikeBooster
Description_EN: Built from parts I collected around The Ewer. The red trail it emits is mesmerising and reminds me of the red cloth of the Ibexii.

Name: LightningCrystalBikeFront
Description_EN: Active rivulets of lightning run in seams within the crystals I harvested in Hakoa. A worthwhile reward for such a dangerous task.

Name: LightningCrystalBikeWings
Description_EN: Lighting is an uncommon power source across the dunes, impractical and dangerous, only those following specific Hakoan Hicaric traditions would even try to utilise it.

Name: LightningCrystalBikeBooster
Description_EN: Lightning filled crystals move themselves using some kind of static charge. The bike controls and guides their formations to harness the power in each crystal with extreme precision.

Name: MonumentalRingBikeFront
Description_EN: Glowing rings pulse with perpetual energy. Remnants of technology used by the Hicaris to harness the power of the Perpetual remain.

Name: MonumentalRingBikeWings
Description_EN: Rings spin in counter movements to create a finely balanced suspension in the air.

Name: MonumentalRingBikeBooster
Description_EN: A booster made from old schematics in Hicaric ruins. The Hicaris must have used similar designs to get around.

Name: NomadicFront
Description_EN: A sleek, minimal look that has a well balanced weight to it. A commonly used part used by many people across Midden’s dunes.

Name: NomadicWings
Description_EN: Floating weights inside each module adjust their position automatically to correct poor driving movements, making for an easy drive.

Name: NomadicBooster
Description_EN: A modern Limina bike component, often seen driven by nomads in the Wash. The fins help create a balanced driving experience.

Name: SandcutterFront
Description_EN: The most recognisable type of bike for children in the dunes, it doesn't get far off the ground or go very fast so it's good to learn on.

Name: SandcutterWings
Description_EN: It's falling apart, but it's familiarity is heart warming. Keep the bike low to the ground and safe for inexperienced drivers, just watch out for rocks.

Name: SandcutterBooster
Description_EN: An old beat up thing, I'm surprised it runs at all. The smoke it outputs is almost definitely toxic.

Name: SpeedsterBikeFront
Description_EN: A recent Machinist development, you can see how the sleek lines of the machined metal deflect wind to create incredible speed.

Name: SpeedsterBikeWings
Description_EN: Small fins flutter like gills, creating small air flows that allow subtle controlled movements at high speed.

Name: SpeedsterBikeBooster
Description_EN: A large fin at the back lowers to provide fantastic aerodynamics. The small fins allow micro adjustments in turning and drag for discrete control.

Name: AtomicPriesthoodSetMask
Description_EN: Description for the atomic priesthood mask

Name: AtomicSuitHelmet
Description_EN: The visor of this Mask gives clarity and visibility I'm not used to with other masks. The air tastes cleaner whilst I wear it.

Name: ChildhoodSetMask
Description_EN: A blank looking mask worn by all children of the dunes. Most kids customise theirs for fun, but more traditional clans frown on that sort of self expression.

Name: ClimbingSetMask
Description_EN: You can't help but feel embodied by a reptilian creature when wearing this mask, walls look less steep somehow.

Name: EngineerSetHelmet
Description_EN: Made from rough parts of old ships beaten into a smooth perfect surface, a mask beautiful because of its functional qualities.

Name: EntertainersMask
Description_EN: A somewhat ridiculous looking mask, but I guess that's the point. I feel a levity looking at it. Wearing it in almost any situation makes the world feel a little warmer.

Name: FishingSetMask
Description_EN: Fish shaped and made of a rubbery material, it's colouration a clear nod to Coral and Cobalt, the twin fish gods.

Name: GuardMask
Description_EN: A mask with 3 feathers from an Eagle, worn by guards in Eccria mostly, but occasionally seen out in the dunes in special circumstances.

Name: HerculesBeetleMask
Description_EN: A mask made to resemble a hercules beetle, made from intricately worked husks that feel hard and rock but extremely lightweight.

Name: HuntersMask
Description_EN: A mask worn by hunters, completely useless, the decorations that adorn it seem to be more to impress other people than for anything else.

Name: IbexiiSetMask
Description_EN: A mask worn by those in the Sansee made of bone. The ones particular to the Ibexii are made from the skull of a mountain goat.

Name: ParasiteBeetleMask
Description_EN: A mask resembling an Oranged Ringed Beetle found in the North East of The Wash. People seem to have great affection for the small and curious critters.

Name: SandwormMask
Description_EN: A rancid smelling thing when worn, somehow made from the flesh of a dead sandwyrm. A definite sense of discomfort seems to come over people when wearing it.

Name: ScrappersMask
Description_EN: A scrappers life is filled with danger and solitude. The mask leaves me with a cold, lonely feeling. A scrappers life is often spent in old ships, alone in your thoughts.

Name: ShadeOfEccriaMask
Description_EN: The mask of the Shade of Eccria, when I wear it, I feel transformed into something closer to a spirit, I don't feel human any more.

Name: ShepherdsMask
Description_EN: Worn by shepherds across Midden, Animals seem to respond to this mask with friendliness and comfort.

Name: SlicerBeetleMask
Description_EN: A mask made to resemble a Slicer Beetle, sharply carved mandibles sit at the top of the mask and glint in the light from certain angles. Beetle poo not included.

Name: ChumMask
Description_EN: A gift from the Queen Chum, this mask reacts whenever I’m close to a Chum that hasn’t been found yet.

Name: AtomicPriesthoodSetTop
Description_EN: Worn by the enigmatic Atomic Priesthood, beaten into shape from old ship parts and decorated with cloaks that represent different rankings within the Priesthood

Name: AtomicSuitTop
Description_EN: Made from an unrecognisable material, wearing it seems to keep me at a consistently comfortable temperature.

Name: BeetleSetTop
Description_EN: A hooded top worn by the Beetle Station owners throughout the dunes. I can’t help feeling a sense of familiarity and safety when wearing one.

Name: BikerSetTop
Description_EN: Often worn by bikers from the Redsee, designed to give good movement whilst accelerating across dunes.

Name: BlackDesertSetTop
Description_EN: Made from a rubbery material this is essential gear for Hakoan Crystal Farmers, with plenty of storage for harvested crystals.

Name: ChildhoodSetTop
Description_EN: My old Ibexii top and hood, a hand-me-down from Yaara. The scratchiness reminds me of home.

Name: ClimbingSetTop
Description_EN: Though they won’t help me climb further, the hand wraps and lightweight fit of this gear are great for making scaling mountainous heights more comfortable.

Name: EccrianSetTop
Description_EN: A sophisticated look, suitable for a city dweller. It's not particularly practical but I feel a certain confidence whilst wearing it.

Name: EngineerSetTop
Description_EN: A standard uniform worn by Machinists in Midden, Machinists will always make sure each set has its own unique modifications for their own particular needs.

Name: FingerClanSetTop
Description_EN: Great for climbing atop the Chales, the fur lining gives you great protection for exploring at heights.

Name: FishingSetTop
Description_EN: A bib for fishing.

Name: GuardOutfitTop
Description_EN: Worn by the guards of Eccria, provides protection against physical attacks whilst retaining mobility.

Name: IbexiiSetTop
Description_EN: The clothing made for Ibexii children as they go out on their gliding. The cloak is unmistakably Ibexii red.

Name: MonumentalTop
Description_EN: An incredibly crafted piece of ritual clothing. It is awkward to wear but hard to take your eyes off.

Name: SurferSetTop
Description_EN: Clothing particularly well suited for sliding down large sand dunes.

Name: WhiteDesertSetTop
Description_EN: Worn by those who live in the Wash, the material is permeable making it naturally cooling and comfortable to wear in the sun.

Name: AtomicPriesthoodSetTrousers
Description_EN: The way these trousers sound when an Atomic Priest approaches is unmistakable. They are hot and uncomfortable to wear in the sandy dunes.

Name: AtomicSuitTrousers
Description_EN: So lightweight and form fitting that it doesn't feel much more than an extra layer of skin. Whatever it is made of is unclear to me.

Name: BeetleSetTrousers
Description_EN: Soft and luxurious, they remind me of a good nights sleep.

Name: BikerSetTrousers
Description_EN: Padding inside the legs of these makes sure they don't wear thin when riding bikes constantly over long distances at high speeds.

Name: BlackDesertSetTrousers
Description_EN: Rubberised to insulate the wearer from static charges and lightning strikes, these clothes are perfect for exploring the rough Hakoan landscape.

Name: ChildhoodSetTrousers
Description_EN: Comfy sandles for exploring sandy places. Easy to get sand out of but not the best for climbing.

Name: ClimbingSetBottoms
Description_EN: Tight foot wrappings allow you to feel every nook and foothold as you climb. It doesn’t make you more effective at climbing but it does feel better somehow.

Name: EccrianSetTrousers
Description_EN: A simple pair of trousers worn in the Eccrine style. Great for an evening city stroll down by the oasis or for relaxing at the Overhang Cafe.

Name: EngineerSetTrousers
Description_EN: Practical trousers for working on bikes and other atomic gear, pockets for tools everywhere.

Name: FingerClanSetTrousers
Description_EN: The split toe shoes make missteps at great heights less likely, and grips on the soles make exploration easier on foot.

Name: FishingSetTrousers
Description_EN: Boots and trousers for fishing, they seem to reduce the noise made on sandy surfaces, so anglers don't scare away sandfish, with lots of pockets for bait.

Name: GuardOutfitBottoms
Description_EN: Made from Hakoan metals, the lightness of these metals is unparalleled, allowing nimble movement and strong resistance in combat.

Name: IbexiiSetTrousers
Description_EN: Jadi made these, the patterning is unique to the Ibexii. The fabric seems to have been made at the Burnt Oak Station.

Name: MonumentalBottom
Description_EN: They have somehow withstood the test of time, avoiding centuries of sand blasting it into a smooth nothingness, this ritual item glow magnificently.

Name: SableHunchTrousers
Description_EN: Supremely comfortable, but make sure the top is tied up tightly before venturing into sandy terrain, sand will build up and weigh you down otherwise.

Name: SurferSetTrousers
Description_EN: Layered shorts that flap satisfyingly when surfing down a sand dune at high speeds, they work well at keeping grains of sand out.

Name: WhiteDesertSetTrousers
Description_EN: Often worn by traders, these trousers are made of a material that is resistant to the stains of the chalk deposits that litter The Wash.

Name: ScrapMetal
Description_EN: Scrap metal collected by scrappers for use in a variety of ways, from repairing bikes and other mechanised goods to producing cuts. Scavenged from ancient ships and can be traded at the scrap yard at Burnt Oak Station.

Name: ClimbingBadge
Description_EN: Shaped like a reptile's hand, I feel more confident about climbing just looking at it.

Name: FallowPomegranate
Description_EN: Only worth picking after they have fermented, these Pomegranate create a delicious tasting beverage.

Name: ShyRijwurPulp
Description_EN: Emits from the top of the Shy Rijwur plant. It is remarkably sticky. Very helpful when repairing things.

Name: Fig
Description_EN: Undoubtedly my favourite fruit, the first thing I would ask for when we passed travelling merchants with the Ibexii.

Name: ShadedLeafButterfly
Description_EN: Found in the palm forest in the western dunes, these vibrant creatures help to propagate life in the forest.

Name: ChalkButterfly
Description_EN: Found in the Wash, the white parts of its wings seem to be from a build up of chalk, allowing you to tell the age of a butterfly by how white it is.

Name: OasisDragonfly
Description_EN: Only found in large, open bodies of water. Tricky to catch but beautiful to look at and extremely nutritious.

Name: SaltButterfly
Description_EN: These butterflies seem to feed off particular plants in the salt plains, it’s a wonder that they can survive out there at all.

Name: SunshineButterfly
Description_EN: Butterflies that hibernate during the cold desert nights by burrowing into the sands. During the day they fly in the dunes, actively seeking sunlight to absorb into their wings in a process akin to photosynthesis.

Name: MerchantBadge
Description_EN: A gem representing a cut adorns this circular badge, given or sold by members of the merchants guild.

Name: BadlandsMap
Description_EN: Impossible to navigate through the towering and maze-like cliffs without, a map of the Badlands made and sold by members of the Cartography guild

Name: EasternDuneMap
Description_EN: A map of the Sansee. We Ibexii spend most of our time in these scorching sands, certain landmarks are familiar but this map of the Sansee will help for the vast lands between. Made and sold by members of the Cartography guild

Name: WesternDuneMap
Description_EN: A map of the Redsee made and sold by members of the Cartography guild.

Name: WhiteDesertMap
Description_EN: A map of The Wash. Chalky deposits punctuate pink sands. The map is made and sold by members of the Cartography guild

Name: BlackDesertMap
Description_EN: A map of Hakoa, this will be useful navigating its dense and unforgiving atmosphere. Created and traded by members of the Cartography guild

Name: SaltPlainsMap
Description_EN: A map of the flat, metal littered Sodic Waste made and sold by members of the Cartography guild

Name: CatEarCanyonMap
Description_EN: A map of the Ewer sold by members of the Cartography guild

Name: EntertainersBadge
Description_EN: I remember making these as a child. It's not the most well made badge you'll ever see but it has a certain spirit to it.

Name: ThievingMagpiesWellDoorKey
Description_EN: A key given to me by Micah to open doors in the Thieving Magpie Well

Name: DuskFirefly
Description_EN: Small luminescent creatures that appear in the desert night. They only live for one day, a day spent frantically laying eggs in cacti that take a week to gestate.

Name: MelancholyMushroom
Description_EN: Found in the Petrified Forest in The Wash. They taste quite nice despite the miserable name.

Name: PricklyPear
Description_EN: Somehow manifested from bones of creatures out in the Wash, extremely tasty to eat but difficult to swallow due to the seeds that permeate the fruit.

Name: CrystalButterfly
Description_EN: These butterflies lay eggs in clusters of crystals, they seem to draw energy from the crystaline forms. Other butterflies struggle in the same conditions.

Name: CactusSpineRod
Description_EN: Made from a cactus spine, the pliability of this rod makes it excellent to fish with.

Name: TricksterAtomicRod
Description_EN: An extremely light and strong rod. Fishing is a breeze with this thing.

Name: BeetleBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Beetle Bike.

Name: CrazyBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for some very wacky Bike colours.

Name: AtomicCorePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for Atomic colours.

Name: AtomicShellPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Atomic Shell Bike.

Name: AzurePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Azure Bike.

Name: BeetleHuskPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Beetle Husk Bike.

Name: DustyMonumentPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Dusty Monument Bike.

Name: EccrineGreenPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Eccrine Green Bike.

Name: HakoanBlackPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Hakoan Black Bike.

Name: IbexiiRedPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Ibexii Red Bike.

Name: LavenderFlashPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Lavender Flash Bike.

Name: NaturesGreenPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for Natures Green colours.

Name: NeonFlashPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Neon Flash Bike.

Name: PyraustaGreyPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Pyrausta Grey Bike.

Name: SaltPlainPoolsPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for Salt Plain Pools colours.

Name: SandyPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Sandy Bike.

Name: StrawPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Straw Bike.

Name: SunsetsPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for Sunsets colours.

Name: WashBluePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for Wash Blue colours.

Name: WindChimePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Wind Chime Bike.

Name: DeliveryBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Delivery Bike.

Name: EyriesBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Eyries Bike.

Name: GiraffeBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Giraffe Bike.

Name: GlidingBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Gliding Bike.

Name: LightningCrystalBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Lightning Crystal Bike.

Name: MonumentalBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Monumental Bike.

Name: AtomicWhaleShipBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Whaleship Bike.

Name: NomadicBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Nomadic Bike.

Name: RedBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Red Bike.

Name: SandcutterBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Sandcutter Bike.

Name: SpeedsterBikePalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Speedster Bike.

Name: LarvalHuskPackage
Description_EN: Ifaa wants me to deliver this to Aeyan in Eccria.

Name: PinkNeckEggs
Description_EN: Extremely rare eggs with a pink dusting. Laid by Pink Neck's up high in the geyser rocks of the salt plains to protect their young. The warm, moist air from the geysers are essential for a successful hatching.

Name: ScrappersBadge
Description_EN: Scrapper Altyn gave me this. I can trade it for a Scrapper's Mask.

Name: Compass
Description_EN: A device given to me by Jadi, it helps me find my way as I explore.

Name: CartographersMask
Description_EN: A mask befitting the cartographer's guild. The metal instruments pivoting around the mask function to allow Cartographers to calibrate devices used to map out unexplored areas.

Name: MerchantsMask
Description_EN: The masks all the merchants in Midden wear.

Name: ChumTear
Description_EN: A tear gifted to me by the Chum Queen for bringing her Chum Eggs. The tear entered my stomach when it was given to me, filling me with a warm strength, I feel stronger and my stamina improves with each tear.

Name: AtomicHeartSmashedGlass
Description_EN: Fragments of smashed glass discovered inside the Atomic Heart. Strangely, the glass seemed to be smashed from the inside as most of the glass landed outside the central chamber.

Name: AtomicHeartDebtNote
Description_EN: A note found inside the Atomic Heart, it indicates a debt owed to some kind of Merchant. Not a group to be trifled with at the best of times.

Name: AtomicHeartFeather
Description_EN: Large feathers that clearly belong to a bird of considerable size. They were scattered inside in the Atomic Heart core chamber - a potential lead?

Name: AtomicHeartEmptySocket
Description_EN: The empty socket where the Atomic Core was. Damage on it was completely superficial, something about it feels like a set up.

Name: AtomicHeartPowerCoreBroken
Description_EN: An broken powercore remnant - extrenely valuable when operational, this unit should be taken to the Machinist in Eccria for repair so it can be used to bring power back to Eccria.

Name: AtomicHeartPowerCoreRepaired
Description_EN: Repaired by Machinist Hamza in Eccria, this powercore should be taken to the Atomic Heart so that it can be used to restore power back to Eccria.

Name: AtomicHeartSuspectGaray
Description_EN: Garay lives in the Serai, just beneath the entrance to the town of Eccria. He wears a Climbers mask and he seems to give off an unpleasant odour.

Name: AtomicHeartSuspectIria
Description_EN: Iria is a Merchant that leads illicit Black Market operations. Her office is found above the Beetle Market in Eccria. It's not easy to be granted an audience with her but her guard is known to be susceptable to a monetary incentive.

Name: AtomicHeartSuspectHamza
Description_EN: The town Machinist, Hamza. He is most often found in his workshop beneath the two wind catcher structures in Eccria. His workshop appeared ramsacked when I first visited.

Name: CartographersBadge
Description_EN: A badge traded by Cartographers, it's often quite a journey to get to them but the views are incredible and they are always worth speaking to about the local area.

Name: StepwellDragonfly
Description_EN: Often found sat cooling on stepwell lillypads, these dragonflies seem to struggle outside of the unique climate of a stillwater well environment.

Name: ChumTop
Description_EN: A top gifted to me by the Chum Queen for finding all her Chum Eggs. Designed in a way I've never seen other clothes made. I wonder who made it?

Name: ChumBottoms
Description_EN: A skirt gifted to me by the Chum Queen for finding all her Chum Eggs. Who made it? It is light as a feather, and dazzles onlookers when worn.

Name: ShadeSetTop
Description_EN: A tight fitting top that belonged to the original Shade of Eccria. It has a fabric material under the arms used to create a unique, imposing silhouette.

Name: ShadeSetTrousers
Description_EN: Trousers worn by the Shade of Eccria, before I was even born. They are especially quiet, great for sneaking up on unsuspecting guards.

Name: ShadeOfEccriaFront
Description_EN: An old bike front with a unique design, created many moons ago in Eccria. It gives me the creeps if I look at it too long.

Name: ShadeOfEccriaWings
Description_EN: Small wings jut from the side of the bike, functionally useless.

Name: ShadeOfEccriaBooster
Description_EN: A rotating mechanism at the back of the bike built to look much like an eye, a reminder to those watching the bike from behind that the Shade sees all.

Name: AnglerBadge
Description_EN: It's a badge from the Angler.

Name: FlutterThrust
Description_EN: Found in the Fingers, these delicate creatures avoid predators by blending their fins into the striations of nearby rocks.

Name: DopeyDart
Description_EN: Found across Sansee, these goofy looking fish are extremely quick but remarkably unintelligent. A bland but nutritious offering on plates across Midden.

Name: ShelledCup
Description_EN: Nobody knows how these fish survive in the harsh salted landscape of the Sodic Waste but without them a delicate ecosystem that sustains creatures like Pink Necks would be disrupted.

Name: Hummer
Description_EN: A bird-like fish that often surfaces from below sands to consume food in it’s natural habitat of Palm Forests. They move with erratic and incredible speed.

Name: SuckerFish
Description_EN: The long mouth of these creatures means they inhale a significant amount of sand, which is then filtered for insects and other nutrients before being spat back out with force that will deter predators.

Name: GhostSoul
Description_EN: These ethereal creatures are impossible to see in some light, and only seem to appear near the corpses of dead creatures, leading people to believe they are somehow connected to the afterlife.

Name: FlutterThrustClue
Description_EN: In the heart of Midden, below pillars of rock.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: DopeyDartClue
Description_EN: Extremely common in the Sansees.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: ShelledCupClue
Description_EN: Seems this creature feeds on Salt.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: HummerClue
Description_EN: These fish seek green areas with trees and foliage.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: SuckerFishClue
Description_EN: Seek the shade of large rock arches in Redsee.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: GhostSoulClue
Description_EN: Seek the souls of old bones.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: StormFishClue
Description_EN: A tricky catch that rides in the crests of crimson sands.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: BellowGuppyClue
Description_EN: Common in the sands of Midden. Hard to miss.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: PillFishClue
Description_EN: Persistence will pay off when fishing in areas full of cacti.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: LittleKickerClue
Description_EN: A common find in pink coloured dunes.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: SandRayClue
Description_EN: In Redsee, these fish seek the deeper parts of the sands.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: TeacupAnglerClue
Description_EN: Rumour is these creatures feed on gaseous substances.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: ScabbyFishClue
Description_EN: A common find in the sand paths between towering cliffs.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: TrifleJellyClue
Description_EN: Likes pale mounds of chalk.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: LightKoiClue
Description_EN: A rare find, these will often feed on shrubbery in the Badlands.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: BloatedEyeClamClue
Description_EN: A parasitic tree killer.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: PebbleEyeClue
Description_EN: A common fish that seeks shade in the Eastern Dunes.
ShopDescription_EN: A clue on how to catch a specific fish.

Name: DragonflyBikeFront
Description_EN: An unusual bike part modelled to look like a dragonfly.

Name: DragonflyBikeWings
Description_EN: Wings modelled after a dragonfly, they seem to intimidate anyone who sees them.

Name: DragonflyBikeBooster
Description_EN: A long tail with a streamlined, ergonomic form based on a Dragonfly.

Name: CartoBikeFront
Description_EN: Created from materials salvaged from Cartographer balloons, the front of this bike is extermely lightweight.

Name: CartoBikeWings
Description_EN: A cross-like fin below the wings skims the sand below the bike.

Name: CartoBikeBooster
Description_EN: Cartographers don't tend to use hoverbikes, so the technology used in this novel and impractical design is somewhat unusual.

Name: SaltBikeFront
Description_EN: A hefty bike module used to counter balance the other industrial components of bikes used by salt famers.

Name: SaltBikeWings
Description_EN: Heavy wings, used by salt farmers in the Sodic Waste. Gives bikes a heavy weighting.

Name: SaltBikeBooster
Description_EN: The large wheel on the back of this bike is used in the processing of salt gathered from salt pools.

Name: AnglerBikeFront
Description_EN: Designed to store fishing gear or larger catches, the frontage of this bike also cuts through wind in such a way that the bike produces as little noise as possible.

Name: AnglerBikeWings
Description_EN: Metal protrusions used for hanging fish when Anglers navigate the dunes of Midden.

Name: AnglerBikeBooster
Description_EN: A sleek and elegant layered booster that is quiet so as not to disturb fish beneath the sands.

Name: ScrapperBikeFront
Description_EN: A piece of scrap metal hangs below this module to create a deceptively well weighted ride.

Name: ScrapperBikeWings
Description_EN: Heavy metallic wings tuck away when boosting to create an aerodynamic form.

Name: ScrapperBikeBooster
Description_EN: Crafted from scraps salvaged from old ships, the wings on the back of this booster flatten to keep the back of the bike balanced.

Name: DragonFlySetTop
Description_EN: A flamboyant top with wings that flutter when I jump.

Name: DragonFlySetTrousers
Description_EN: Colourful clothing that feels great to wear.

Name: ScrapperSetTop
Description_EN: Top worn by expert scrappers, with convenient gear for collecting scrap and exploring the abandoned ships of Midden.

Name: ScrapperSetTrousers
Description_EN: Bottoms designed with ship exploring in mind.

Name: DragonFlyPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Dragonfly Bike.

Name: ExtractorPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Salt Extractor Bike.

Name: ScrapperPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Scrapper Bike.

Name: CartographerPalette
Description_EN: The dye bottle for the colours of the Cartographer Bike.
